methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Gauss Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €9M to date. The valuation universe for frontier nuclear comprises 11 comparable entities: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set density. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private comparables total, and filtering by stage (Series A), adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints—below the threshold for statistical reliability. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of capital requirements and timelines in nuclear subsectors.

Without a sufficiently robust peer set or disclosed transaction multiples, revenue, or cash-burn metrics for Gauss Fusion, a triangulated valuation range cannot be established from the data provided. Future analysis should await either: (i) public filings or funding announcements from peer companies that would densify the Series A nuclear cohort, (ii) disclosed unit economics or development milestones for Gauss Fusion itself, or (iii) sector-specific precedent transactions. The stage and capital intensity of nuclear fusion and fission development create wide uncertainty bands around any single-method estimate at this juncture.
